div1.entryFree:
Bargain, vb., 1) to stipulate: 'tis --ed'twixt us twain, that she shall still be curst in company, Shr. II, 307.
2) followed by for == to make an agreement about the transfer of sth.: so worthless peasants b. for their wives, H6A V, 5, 53. while his own lands are --ed for and sold, H6B I, 1, 231. I have --ed for the joint, Per. IV, 2, 141.
